bids.reasoning: At t=0.0, there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueF, which is very low utility for me and gives no evidence of meaningful concession yet, so I will anchor on my strongest values across all issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is extremely early (t=0.02) and the opponent has simply repeated an all-valueF offer, which is very low for me and shows no real concession yet. To avoid exactly repeating my previous all-valueA bid while still protecting my high-weight preferences, I make only a small concession on issueC from valueA to valueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not yet made meaningful concessions. Their offers remain extremely low-value to me, with only a tiny movement on issueD, and there are no messages providing credible additional information. I avoid repeating my last exact bid while making only a small concession on issueC, which is not my highest-weight issue.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early (t=0.06) and the opponent has not made meaningful concessions toward my preferences; their offers remain clustered near my low-value outcomes. I am making a small concession on issueC while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueD, which are important and/or costly for me to concede, and I avoid repeating my previous exact bid.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers remain far below my preferences, with only slight movement on issueC and issueD. I will hold firm on my most important issueA and on issueD, while making a modest tradeoff on issueC and issueB to avoid repeating my last exact bid and test whether the opponent’s recent movement on issueC can be developed.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.78 because it is very early at t=0.10 and the opponent has only recently improved meaningfully, mainly on my most important issueA. Their offers remain weak for me on issueB, issueC, and issueD, so I will hold firm on issueA and issueD while varying issueC upward from my last bid to avoid repeating exactly.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.74, since it is early at t=0.12 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s offers remain low for me overall, but they have shown some movement on issueA, issueB, and once on issueC, so I am conceding on issueB and issueC while holding firm on my most important issueA and valuable issueD.

At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility for myself while making selective concessions on issueB and issueC. The opponent has shown some willingness to move on issueC and once on issueB, but their offers are still low for me overall, so I am holding firm on my highest-priority issueA and on issueD.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a visible concession away from my ideal on issueA. The opponent has repeatedly offered poor overall bids for me, but they have signaled some willingness around issueA: valueB, so I will use that concession while holding firm on high-value outcomes for the other issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.78 because it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent’s offers remain low-utility for me with only limited concession. The opponent has repeatedly shown willingness to include issueA:valueB and has sometimes moved issueC away from valueF, so I concede on issueA and issueC while holding firm on issueB and issueD, which remain valuable to me.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.79 because it is still early at t=0.20 and the opponent’s offers remain low-value to me with only limited concession. The opponent has shown some willingness on issueA=valueB and occasional movement on issueC, so I keep the important issueD at valueA and offer a modestly different high-value package without repeating my last bid.

At t=0.22 it is still early enough to target a fairly strong utility while making some visible concessions from my ideal bid. The opponent has repeatedly pushed low-value outcomes for issueB and issueC and has only occasionally offered better issueA values, so I am conceding moderately on issueB/issueC while holding issueD at my top value and keeping issueA at valueB rather than dropping further.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.68, since t=0.24 is early enough to avoid major concessions but late enough to keep exploring tradeoffs. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and mostly emphasize valueE/valueF, with only occasional movement on issueA, issueB, and issueC; I will hold issueD at my top value while conceding moderately on issueA, issueB, and issueC. No natural-language messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, which is still appropriate at t=0.26 because there is time left and the opponent’s offers remain very low-value to me overall. The opponent has shown some movement on issueA and recently offered issueC: valueB, so I can incorporate valueB on issueC while holding firmer on high-importance issueA and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.75 by holding my most important issueA at valueA and keeping issueD at valueA, while conceding moderately on issueB and issueC. At t=0.28 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s very low B/C/D pattern; their latest offer improved issueA to valueA, but they remain firm on weak values for the other issues.

I am targeting a moderate utility concession around 0.65: I keep issueD at my top value and issueC high, while conceding issueB to valueF and issueA to valueB, both of which the opponent has repeatedly signaled. At t=0.30 there is still time, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet, but their repeated bids with issueB=valueF and recent issueA=valueB suggest this tradeoff may be more acceptable than my earlier high-demand bids.

At t=0.32 I am still targeting a fairly strong utility bid while making visible concessions on issues where the opponent has shown movement, especially issueB and issueC. The opponent’s latest offer improved issueB to valueB and issueC to valueC, so I mirror those while holding issueD at valueA because it remains valuable to me and they have not yet shown acceptable movement there.

I am targeting a moderate concession around the high-0.6 utility range, since t=0.34 is still early-mid negotiation and there is no need to drop close to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueB toward valueE/F and issueD toward valueE/F, but occasionally gives better issueA or issueC values; I will concede somewhat on issueB and issueC while holding issueD at my top value and issueA at a strong value.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.60 by keeping issueA at a strong value while making concessions on the lower-weight issues. At t=0.36 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s very low-value pattern, and their offers show possible overlap on issueA=valueB, issueB=valueB, and some movement on issueC. I am avoiding invalid values and making a fresh compromise rather than repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.54, conceding more than my previous bid because we are moving into the mid-game but still not near the deadline. The opponent’s latest bid already offered issueA:valueB and issueC:valueD, and they have previously shown willingness on issueB:valueB, so I will accept their apparent preference on issueD while asking for a better issueB value.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.57, conceding meaningfully from my early best bids while still protecting the high-weight issueA. The opponent has mostly held firm on low values but has signaled possible flexibility on issueA=valueB, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC/D, and issueD=valueD/E, so I am using those signals while avoiding repeating my previous exact bid.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.63: better than the opponent’s latest offer to me, but with concessions on issueD and issueC where they have repeatedly pushed away from my top values. The opponent’s offers have improved recently, especially by offering issueA: valueA and issueC: valueD, so I will build on those signals while holding more firmly on issueB, where accepting valueE would be too costly for me at t=0.42.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.64, conceding on lower-cost issues while protecting issueA, my highest-weight issue. The opponent has shown some movement on issueB and issueC, and their recent offers include issueB:valueC and issueC:valueD, both acceptable to me; I will match those while offering a concession on issueD as well.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.60, since t=0.46 is not late enough to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers but it is time to make meaningful tradeoffs. The opponent’s bids have occasionally included issueA:valueB, issueB:valueC, issueC:valueD, and most recently issueD:valueB, so this bid incorporates several values they have signaled while protecting my higher-weight issueA from deeper concessions.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.57: meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids, but still well above the weak utility of the opponent’s recent offers. The opponent has not shown steady concession, but they have repeatedly offered issueA=valueB, issueB=valueD, issueC=valueC/valueD, and issueD=valueB/D/E, so I am combining values they have signaled with preserving value on my most important issueA.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.57, conceding further on the lower-weight issues while still protecting issueA, which is my most important issue. The opponent’s recent bids have shown some movement toward values like issueB: valueB and issueD: valueF, so I am incorporating those signals without accepting their still-low overall offers to me.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around the mid-game rather than holding to my earlier high demands, since time is now past the halfway point but not yet at the deadline. The opponent’s latest offer already conceded issueB to my top value and issueA to valueD, while still holding issueC and issueD low for me, so I am keeping their signaled values on issueA/issueB/issueD but asking for a better issueC that they have previously offered.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.52, conceding somewhat from my last offer but not dropping all the way to the opponent’s latest low-utility bid. The opponent has gradually moved away from all-low values and recently offered issueA:valueD, issueB:valueA/valueB, and issueC:valueD, so I combine those signals while conceding issueD to valueE. There are no opponent messages to weigh, so I rely only on the offer history.

I am targeting a mid-level utility around 0.48, conceding further than my last bid but not fully accepting the opponent’s latest repeated position yet. The opponent has recently converged on issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueD, issueD=valueE, so I will match most of that signal while trying to improve issueD slightly for myself at this mid-game time.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.45, conceding further because we are past the midpoint but not yet at the deadline. The opponent has repeatedly signaled willingness around issueA=valueD, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueD, issueD=valueE, including offering that exact combination recently, while their latest move worsened issueC for me. With no messages to consider, I am relying on the offer pattern and proposing a bid they have already shown they can make.
